# Hostaway Design System

A comprehensive React component library built with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and UntitledUI. Provides a consistent, accessible, and modern UI foundation for Hostaway applications.

## 🎯 What's Included

- **Comprehensive Component Library** - Buttons, forms, navigation, data display, and more
- **UntitledUI Foundation** - Built on proven design patterns and components
- **TypeScript Support** - Full type definitions for better developer experience  
- **Tailwind CSS Integration** - Utility-first styling with theme customization
- **Accessibility First** - WCAG compliant components with proper ARIA support
- **Design Tokens** - Consistent colors, spacing, typography, and breakpoints
- **Storybook Documentation** - Interactive component playground and examples

## 📦 Package Information

- **Package**: [`@hostaway/design-system`](https://github.com/Hostaway/design-system/pkgs/npm/design-system)
- **Registry**: GitHub Packages
- **Bundle Size**: Optimized with tree-shaking support (CommonJS + ESM)

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Step 1: Authentication Setup

Since this package is hosted on **GitHub Packages**, you'll need a GitHub Personal Access Token:

1. **Generate a token:**
   - Visit [GitHub Settings → Personal Access Tokens](https://github.com/settings/tokens)
   - Click "Generate new token (classic)"
   - Select `read:packages` scope
   - Copy the generated token

2. **Configure npm authentication:**
   ```bash
   # In your project root, create .npmrc (safe to commit)
   echo "@hostaway:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com" >> .npmrc
   echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=\${GH_PACKAGES_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc
   ```

   > ✅ **Safe to commit**: Uses environment variable, no secrets in the file!

### Step 2: Installation

```bash
# Set your GitHub token as environment variable
export GH_PACKAGES_TOKEN=your_github_token_here

# Install the design system (subscribes to patch updates)
yarn add @hostaway/design-system@~1.0.0
```

### Step 3: Setup Tailwind CSS

The design system uses custom utility classes (like `text-quaternary`, `text-fg-quaternary`, etc.) that map to CSS variables. Configure Tailwind to scan both your app and the design system bundle:

#### Tailwind v4

Add to your `src/styles/tailwind.css` (or equivalent):

```css
@import "tailwindcss/theme.css" layer(theme);
@import "tailwindcss/utilities.css" layer(utilities);

/* Import design system theme variables */
@import "@hostaway/design-system/dist/theme.css" layer(theme);

/* Import tailwind plugins */
@plugin "@hostaway/design-system/tailwind-plugin";

/* Scan your app AND the design system bundle */
@source "../**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}";
@source "../../node_modules/@hostaway/design-system/dist/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}";
```

> **💡 Note:** Adjust the relative paths based on your CSS file location. The paths above assume your CSS file is in `src/styles/`.
> **⚠️ Why This is Required:** The design system uses custom utilities that reference CSS variables. Without scanning the bundled files, Tailwind won't detect these custom class names and they'll be missing from your compiled CSS.

### Step 4: Use Components

```typescript
import { Button, Card, InputField, Badge } from '@hostaway/design-system';

function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <Card className="p-6">
      <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-4">Welcome to Hostaway</h2>
      <InputField 
        label="Property Name" 
        placeholder="Enter property name" 
        className="mb-4"
      />
      <div className="flex gap-2">
        <Button variant="primary">Save Property</Button>
        <Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>
      </div>
    </Card>
  );
}
```

## 🔧 TypeScript Support

Full TypeScript definitions included:

```typescript
import type { ButtonProps, CardProps } from '@hostaway/design-system';

interface MyComponentProps {
  primaryAction: ButtonProps;
  content: CardProps['children'];
}
```

## 🎯 Theming & Customization

The design system uses Tailwind CSS with design tokens. You can override any design token by defining custom `@theme` rules after importing the design system theme:

```css
/* src/styles/tailwind.css */
@import "tailwindcss/theme.css" layer(theme);
@import "tailwindcss/utilities.css" layer(utilities);

/* Import base theme */
@import "@hostaway/design-system/dist/theme.css" layer(theme);

/* Override design tokens */
@theme {
  --color-primary-50: #eff6ff;
  --color-primary-500: #3b82f6;
  --color-primary-900: #1e3a8a;
  
  --font-body: "Inter", ui-sans-serif, system-ui;
  --spacing: 0.25rem;
}

/* Scan sources */
@source "../**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}";
@source "../../node_modules/@hostaway/design-system/dist/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}";
```

> **💡 Tip:** Place your `@theme` overrides after importing the design system theme to override specific tokens while keeping the rest intact.

## 🚨 Troubleshooting

### "Package not found" Error

- Verify your `.npmrc` file contains the correct registry and token
- Ensure your GitHub token has `read:packages` permission
- Check that you're in the same organization/have access to the private package

### TypeScript Errors

- Make sure you're importing from the correct path: `@hostaway/design-system`
- Verify your TypeScript version is compatible (>= 4.0)

### Styling Issues

- **Missing custom classes** (like `text-quaternary`): Ensure you've added the `@source` directive with glob patterns: `@source "../../node_modules/@hostaway/design-system/dist/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}"`
- **Theme CSS not loading**: Verify you've imported with the layer directive: `@import "@hostaway/design-system/dist/theme.css" layer(theme);`
- **Path issues**: Adjust relative paths based on your CSS file location (examples assume `src/styles/`)
- **Cache issues**: Clear Tailwind's cache if changes aren't appearing: `rm -rf .turbo node_modules/.cache`
- **Verify installation**: Make sure the design system package is installed in `node_modules`

## 🔒 Security & CI/CD

For production deployments and CI/CD pipelines:

```bash
# .npmrc (safe to commit to version control)
@hostaway:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GH_PACKAGES_TOKEN}
```

Set `GH_PACKAGES_TOKEN` as an environment variable in your deployment pipeline with your GitHub Personal Access Token.

## 🛠️ Development & Contributing

<details>
<summary>Click to expand development information</summary>

### Development Setup

```bash
# Clone and install
git clone https://github.com/Hostaway/design-system.git
cd design-system
yarn --frozen-lockfile

# Start development
yarn dev                # Storybook development server
yarn test               # Run test suite
yarn build              # Build for production
```

### Component Development

- **Storybook**: `http://localhost:6006` - Component playground
- **Testing**: Jest + React Testing Library
- **TypeScript**: Strict mode enabled
- **Linting**: ESLint + Prettier configured
- **Commit linting**: [commitlint](https://commitlint.js.org/) with Conventional Commits

### Commit Convention

This project enforces [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) via **commitlint** + **husky** (`commit-msg` hook). Every commit message must follow this format:

```
type(scope?): description
```

**Allowed types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `build`, `ci`, `chore`, `revert`

Examples:
```bash
git commit -m "feat: add DatePicker component"
git commit -m "fix(Input): resolve focus ring on Safari"
git commit -m "chore(deps): bump tailwindcss to v4.2"
```

A commit with an invalid message will be rejected automatically.

### Steps for adding a new component from UntitledUI
!! This applies for the cases where we don't have the UntitledUI base component in our codebase yet. 

1. Sign in to UntitledUI. The email is available in 1Password under UntitledUI login. 
2. Use #engineering-inbox to check the email with the magic link
3. Find your component in UntitledUI and go to the installation step -> the CLI option
```npx untitledui@latest add <component>```
The code of the component from UntitledUI will be added in the `/vendor/untitled-ui` folder under `base/application/organisms/marketing` depending on the type of component from UntitledUI
4. Create a wrapper for the UntitledUI component by creating a folder in the `components/atoms` or the `components/molecules` folder. You can follow the example from `Checkbox.tsx`
If your component needs customization on top of the code from UntitledUI, add this in your wrapper. Avoid changing the base component code unless it's necessary (even formatting...). 
Try to make the components as easy to use as possible for the developers. 
5. Add the `data-id` prop to interactive components. All the interactive components have a `data-id` so you can check any of them for an example.
6. [TBD]: best practices for translations
7. Create a story for it in the corresponding section Atoms/Molecules in the `stories` folder. The story should include all the variations of the component to offer developers a visualization of all the possible use cases of the component.
Add all the possible props to the documentation of the story in the argTypes of the base story. These will appear at the top of each story and it will help developers see which props are available for use on the component.
8. Export the newly created component in `src/index.ts`. Example: `export { Badge, type BadgeProps } from './components/Badge/Badge';`
9. Bump version if you want to release the changes immediately

### Steps for adding a new icon to the DS
1. Make sure the icon doesn't already exist in UntitledUI
2. Add a wrapper around the SVG path to turn it into a component. Follow the example from MyCustomIcon.tsx
3. Add the icon export in `icons.ts`
4. Import the icon the same as the other icons `import { MyCustomIcon } from '@/components/icons';`

### Releasing a new version
 Bump package version and open a PR against main. Once the PR is merged the workflows will be triggered and a new version of the package will be released

### Deploying the storybook
The Storybook is deployed automatically on ```https://main--6968e7a07f8cde567e87cf06.chromatic.com/``` when merging to main. The deploy is handled by Chromatic.


### Architecture

Built with modern development practices:

- 🏗️ **Rollup** for optimized builds
- 📦 **Dual format** output (CommonJS + ESM)
- 🧪 **Comprehensive testing** with Jest
- 📖 **Storybook** for component documentation
- 🤖 **AI-friendly** with Cursor development rules

See [Architecture.md](./Architecture.md) and [Workflows README](./.github/workflows/README.md) for detailed technical documentation.

</details>
